# Rules Feedback – 25 March 2026
Feedback on `rules_bg.md` (Tableau edition). Mandate/Seat/Promotion sections excluded per review scope.

---

## Contradictions

**Chieftain in building cadence rule (lines 151–152)**
Line 151 groups the Chieftain with Hotelier and Industrialist for the "one more Resource-Generating than Support Buildings" ratio rule. Line 152 immediately says the Chieftain has no such limit. Remove the Chieftain from line 151.

**Votes generated by Industrialist vs. Bureaucrat (line 53 vs. line 131)**
The Resources section says votes are generated by the Industrialist per building adjacent to Infrastructure. But the Infrastructure building (line 131) produces 🗳️ for the Bureaucrat. Should say Bureaucrat, not Industrialist.

**Infrastructure vote rule ambiguous (line 131)**
"Any player with any number of Buildings adjacent to Infrastructure also receives 1 Vote." — does a non-Bureaucrat player get exactly 1 🗳️ total, or 1 per adjacent building? Also uses "Vote" (spelled out) inconsistently with 🗳️ elsewhere.

---

## Unclear Rules

**Resort Sand+Water bonus (line 125)**
"…and also if the Resort is placed on a Sand and also adjacent to Water." A Resort already earns 💰 for each adjacent Water hex. Does this Sand+Water condition grant a separate/additional bonus (double-dipping on Water), or is something else intended?

**Urban Planning (line 300)**
"Place an additional building on a hex you already control" — does this mean stacking a second building on a hex that already has one? What does "control" mean here? Also, "That hex produces double when you use Liaison's resource-generation option for it" — Liaison generates from all buildings at once; "for it" is vague. Consider: "that building produces double resources."

**Land Claims (line 309)**
"Until your next turn, buildings cannot be placed adjacent to Reserves." — whose Reserves? All Reserves on the map, only yours, or only opponents'? Does this affect all players or just opponents?

**Propaganda (line 303)**
"Pay 1–3 💰 and collect 1–3 🗳️ from other players, taking at most 1 🗳️ from each player." — it's unclear whether the payment and collection are 1:1 (pay 1 get 1 from one player; pay 3 get up to 3 total from 3 players), or whether the two ranges are independent.

**Chieftain mechanics inside Builder card (line 107)**
The Builder card description includes "The Chieftain can use the Elder to place a Village… and a Reserve." The Chieftain doesn't use Builder for this — Elder is a separate card. Move this note to the Elder card or Building Cadence section.

**"3 or 4 🗳️" in Politics (line 289)**
"If a non-Bureaucrat player paid 3 or 4 🗳️ for that card…" — the Politics table only shows slots costing 0–3 🗳️. There is no 4 🗳️ slot visible in this document. Either a slot is missing from the table, or this should just say "3 🗳️."

**Expropriation (line 312)**
"The Industrialist adds 1–3 🪵 and/or ⚙️ to the Resource Market and receives 1 💰." — this card appears in the general Politics deck and could be acquired by any player, but the effect only references the Industrialist. Should it use "you" instead, or is it intentionally Industrialist-only (and if so, how does a non-Industrialist resolve it)?

**Action Token refresh not stated in core rules**
Taking Turns (line 63) never explicitly says tokens are returned to the player at the start of their next turn. Line 157 implies this with "take your two Action Tokens onto your playmat (or refresh them)" but "refresh" is unexplained. Add a sentence to Taking Turns.

**Turn order direction not established early**
Line 164 says "the next Player to your right takes her turn" — this is the only place turn direction is stated, buried near the end of the section. Establish this earlier.

---

## Orphaned / Broken Text

**Floating fragment after Guide (line 117)**
The line "Resort, Industrial Zone, Infrastructure, or Village depending on player type." appears after the Guide card description with no context. Likely a cut-and-paste remnant — delete it.

**Broken markdown link (line 28)**
`Mandate card[Mandate card](#mandate-card)` — malformed link syntax. Should be `[Mandate card](#mandate-card)` or remove the preceding text.

**Wrong code fence syntax (lines 68–73)**
The starting resources block uses `'''` instead of triple backticks. Won't render as a code block in standard Markdown.

---

## Repetition

**Starting Tableau composition stated three times**
Starting cards for each player type are listed at line 31 (Player Types intro), lines 81–88 (Tableaus section), and implicitly again at line 92 (Starting cards intro). Consolidate to one location (Tableaus section is the natural home).

---

## Miscellaneous

**"Resources Track" vs. "Resource Market" (lines 51–52)**
Called "Resources Track" twice in the Resources section; everywhere else it's "Resource Market" or "Resources Market." Pick one name.

**Advocate has no description**
Advocate is listed as one of the four starting Network cards but has no entry in the "Examples of Personnel" section. Add a description or replace it in the setup list.

**Internal design notes in Network section (line 198)**
"(the same idea as Conference in the deck-and-hand version of the rules)" and "The Narrative is the same: staff network at a conference…" read as developer notes, not player-facing rules. Cut from final document.

**Pronoun inconsistency**
Most of the document uses "her" as the generic player pronoun, but line 151 uses "he" for the Chieftain. Pick one and be consistent throughout.
